mission.title: Install and Configure Openfga on Kubernetes
mission.description: OpenFGA is a high-performance and flexible authorization/permission engine built for developers, inspired by Google Zanzibar. Installing OpenFGA allows you to manage fine-grained access control for your applications effectively.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the OpenFGA Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration using the following command:
```bash
helm repo add openfga https://openfga.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install OpenFGA using Helm
steps.description:
Install OpenFGA in a new namespace called 'openfga' with the following command:
```bash
helm install openfga openfga/openfga --namespace openfga --create-namespace --version 0.2.55
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the OpenFGA pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace openfga
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the OpenFGA release with the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall openfga --namespace openfga
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) associated with OpenFGA:
```bash
kubectl delete crd openfgas.openfga.dev
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'openfga' namespace to clean up any remaining resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace openfga
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your current configuration and data. You can export your current resources with:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace openfga -o yaml > openfga-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to get the latest charts:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade OpenFGA
upgrade.description:
Upgrade OpenFGA to the latest version with the following command:
```bash
helm upgrade openfga openfga/openfga --namespace openfga --version 0.2.55
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the OpenFGA pods are running the new version:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace openfga
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your pods are stuck in a Pending state, check for insufficient resources:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace openfga
``` 
Ensure that your cluster has enough resources available.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot reach the OpenFGA service, check the service configuration:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace openfga
``` 
Ensure that the service type is set to 'ClusterIP' or 'LoadBalancer' as needed.

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff error
troubleshooting.description:
If you see a CrashLoopBackOff error, check the logs of the pod:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace openfga
``` 
This may give insights into what is causing the crash.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of OpenFGA will result in all pods running in the 'openfga' namespace without errors. You should be able to access the OpenFGA service as configured.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add openfga https://openfga.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Helm and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.
